channel.title: +Articles on Vishvas's notes
channel.description: Recent content in +Articles on Vishvas's notes

item.title: Houthi rise
item.description:
Source: TW

Thread with excerpts from “Tribes and Politics in Yemen: A History of the Houthi Conflict” by @BrandtMarieke

The concept of “dual use” knowledge

Provinces of Yemen

tribes of Sa’dah province (left) and al-Jawf province (right)

Sheikhs

(Tribal leaders.)

Sheikhs in north Yemen are elected from eligible men from the sheikh’s family. Their authority is largely a product of their role as mediators & arbiters between the clans. They rely heavily on local notables & elders to aide them. State, customary, & sharia law coexist.

item.title: Monotheist transition
item.description:
मानसतरङ्गिणीकृत् - टिप्पनी

The work presented here provides key insights into the rise of marUnmAda (loka-shokasya mahat kAraNaM svajyeShThayA saha ). The authors present the case that the spread of prathamonmAda and dvitIyonmAda in the Arab lands resulted in a first-stage conversion to a “soft” ekarAkShasavAda starting about a century before marUnmAda. This then blew up as the latter with the coming of mahAmada.

What the authors don’t cover is the dynamics of how earlier ekarAkShasonmAda-s spread into Arabia. Some of it is too dangerous to say much about in public even today. But the discerning H can gain information on that. Moreover, the authors also don’t bring in the dynamics of the great Iran-pretonmAda war that was underway as marUnmAda emerged.
